title: microlepia marginalis
intro: Microlepia marginalis, also known as the 'Fern of Life' or 'Gota Kola', is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Microlepia marginalis include reducing fever, relieving respiratory problems, and supporting digestive health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or powders, and can also be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
